author: @edkoetsier339
text: You were ripped off matee. That cpu will only be able to run circa 2005 games. It will chug on windows 10, and anything else is a security hazard. If you don't connect to the internet though, it will be OK to program microcontrollers and run 3d printers and CNC machines, but not much else. You should have gone for a relatively recent 6th gen pc (HP or Dell) which go for peanuts. Those parts are worth 0 and you would have more processing power with a Pi.

It’s all laid out on the download website but I haven’t been able to get it to work. :(

author: @NorthernKitty
text:
Nice job!!  That Windows license probably cost more than your entire computer build, LOL!!  

I see Rob's luck of "finding things" is rubbing off on you!!  Snagging that graphics card was a very lucky find, that alone could've set you back £150!!  That just goes to show that it's worth keeping your eyes open and asking around for people getting rid of things - one person's junk is another's treasure!!  A person can build a very nice system just looking around for old systems and spare parts, as you've done here.  😊

Do you plan on doing much more computer content?  I think that would be a great addition to Rob's channel - especially Arduino or Raspberry Pi applications.  😊  Microcontrollers are a Maker's dream!!  (Although I have to say I'm also enjoying watching Rob constantly show us easier ways to do things with more basic components, as he did the other day with his motor solution using a simple relay.  😋)  It seems you have a knack for it - that's definitely a skill to keep honing, there's never a lack of opportunities in I.T.!!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you! hahaha indeed, All my builds over the years have had parts I have just found/given to me, which is awesome! If you know what you're looking for it is easy to find really good deals (eBay/Facebook/gumtree, etc). I really enjoyed this build which defiantly entices me to do more on this sort of stuff in the future. I agree to do stuff with Arduinos/Raspberry pis, would be AWESOME!

author: @Arachnoid_of_the_underverse
text: Nice build Luke its looks  very unique, you have some excellent friends to donate parts. You might want to add a fan on the back panel if you can, to keep the internals cool.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Yes luckily my friend just got a new laptop so he was getting rid of his old computer parts quite cheap which is awesome for me, I guess it was the right place right time. Searching places like eBay/Facebook/gumtree you can find some really good deals. And for the GPU getting that for nothing was the highlight of my week lol. Thank you mate! :)

author: @ThisRandomUsername
text:
That's a pretty well-balanced build, just right for the price.

A few things:
-Those i5s only have one thread per core, so it's a 4-core, 4-thread processor. Until recently that's all you'd needed for most games. 
-You called the double-4 connector a 6-pin connector. 6-pin (and 6+2 or single 8-pin) power connectors are for typically for graphics cards. You didn't do anything wrong there.
-You should probably try to get at least a 120mm fan exhausting air from the back of the case. You could build up quite a bit of heat from having the GPU and CPU both dumping heat into the case. Also, I've had really good luck ducting air from a front fan into my stock AMD CPU cooler (around 8 degrees cooler), but that could ruin your aesthetic.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I thought these CPUS had more threads but I was wrong lol,  I have 2 fans ready to be put on, I guess I was a bit excited to get the build finished. Get them put on the build should run really cool. Thank you mate! :)
